codeReview.summary: The feature correctly converts posthog-js to dynamic imports in both entry.client.tsx and use-analytics.ts. The entry client now lazy-loads the default export only when PostHog config exists, and use-analytics.ts caches a module-level dynamic import promise so capture calls load the library only on demand while preserving the hook API.

issues.description: The fire-and-forget dynamic import chain has no .catch() handler. If the posthog-js chunk fails to load, this can surface as an unhandled promise rejection. Low impact because analytics is non-critical, but it would be safer to handle rejected imports explicitly in both capture paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission AGENTS.md documents adding .catch() for fire-and-forget promises only with triggerWebhook, but the same void promise pattern is now used for lazy analytics imports. The convention could be generalized for all intentionally unawaited promise chains.

summary: PASS. The dynamic posthog refactor matches the feature requirements and preserves behavior while removing static posthog-js imports from the initial client bundle. I found one non-blocking issue: the new fire-and-forget import chains in use-analytics.ts do not catch rejected imports.
